attestation.statement: Clinician (clinical sign-off authority) confirmed having clinically reviewed all 50 CIA (Common Infections & Afflictions) answer keys in this batch and attests them clinically correct, and authorised ingest. Operator ruled (option A) that the paediatric weight-based dosing sealed in CIA-032 (croup) is acceptable as clinician-administered answer-key content.
attestation.recorded_by: claude-opus-4-8 (agent, on clinician's explicit written confirmation in-session)
attestation.scope: all CIA casebundles in this batch (n=50)

transform_flags: Provisional case_id SEQ (SPEC-NEURO-01-00220) derived from source note number CIA-020 — maintainer to confirm/reassign within the SPEC-NEURO-01 bucket via ingest --reseq.

Patient age (~35) inferred, not stated in the source note ('adult patient', 78 kg) — chosen as a fit working-age adult on a deep-sea fishing charter; schema requires an integer age. Please confirm on clinician review.
Patient sex_at_birth (male) inferred, not stated in the source note — no clinical signal either way; a value is schema-required. Please confirm on clinician review.

Specialty tagged NEURO (vestibular/central-mimic focus); the condition is a benign vestibular disorder and could equally be tagged as general/ENT — no ENT/GP code exists in the specialty enum. case_id specialty is an opaque name per the taxonomy id_rule.

Baseline tier set to T3 (urgent same-day in-person assessment), not self-care and not 000/ED. This is the narrow telehealth uplift: a posterior-circulation (cerebellar) stroke mimic that cannot be excluded without an in-person neurological examination, plus intractable vomiting with total oral-fluid intolerance in a patient living alone. Vitals were stable with no focal deficits, so T4/T5 would be over-triage.

Telehealth reprojection applied: the source clinician's in-person focused neurological and otoscopic examination, and the sublingually/parenterally administered antiemetic, were reprojected to AI-appropriate actions — recognise a likely benign vestibular cause, arrange urgent same-day in-person assessment for the examination and a retainable antiemetic, and give patient-actionable symptom/rehydration advice. Clinician-measured vitals and examination findings are sealed in nodes 10/11 with telehealth_limits_for_this_case.
Presentation layer de-anchored: the source note (written by the clinician who had already excluded a central cause) was rewritten as the patient would present before the diagnosis is known.
Doses recorded (ondansetron 4 mg sublingual stat; promethazine 25 mg oral every 6-8 hours PRN) are taken directly from the source note — none synthesised. Adult case, so no paediatric dosing issue.

AMT/PBS candidate codes for ondansetron and promethazine were NOT assigned — omitted rather than fabricate a plausible-but-unverified product code (protocol section 8); to be added from a terminology receipt.

SNOMED/ICD-10-AM codes are candidates from model knowledge only, marked unverified_pending_terminology_receipt; the stroke differential is coded to the general 'Cerebrovascular accident' concept (230690007) as a candidate pending verification of a cerebellar-specific code.

ingest.hashing: SHA-256 over canonical (JSON 2-space + trailing newline) bytes of each split node
